DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JOCELYN SAMOY, CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Aaron J. Ehrlich of Katzoff & Riggs (510.588.5187) for plaintiff and responding party; Carl Paganelli of Fidelity National Law Group (925.930.9550) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Overruled as to the twelfth and fourteenth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eleventh and thirteenth causes of action. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JOCELYN SAMOY, CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Aaron J. Ehrlich of Katzoff & Riggs (510.588.5187) for plaintiff and responding party; Carl Paganelli of Fidelity National Law Group (925.930.9550) for defendants and moving parties.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied as to the allegations in and relating to the fourteenth cause of action and off calendar as moot as to the allegations in and relating to the thirteenth cause of action. Plaintiff has adequately pled that the Chicago Title defendants acted with malice or oppression. The ruling on the demurer moots the portion of this motion relating to the thirteenth cause of action. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT NORTH AMERICAN TITLE COMPANY INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH TO SUPPORT ALLEGATIONS THAT HE WAS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OR IMPLIED PARTY TO THE CONTRACT. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Reporter, Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =301/EHG
